description_long: This object is a classic percussion cap revolver, featuring a long, blued steel barrel and a six-chambered rotating cylinder. The frame and barrel are constructed from dark metal, while the grip is made of polished wood with a rich brown finish. The trigger guard is brass-toned and rounded, providing a visual contrast to the rest of the firearm. The hammer and trigger are both visible, with the hammer positioned at the rear above the grip. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt revolvers, with a robust and utilitarian appearance. The object is detailed with visible screws and mechanical joints, indicating functional moving parts.
description: A classic percussion cap revolver with a long blued barrel, wooden grip, and brass trigger guard, featuring a six-chambered cylinder and detailed mechanical parts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, wooden grip, and brass trigger guard.
description_view_1: Angled front-top view highlighting the barrel opening, cylinder, and partial grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the barrel, cylinder, and the curvature of the wooden grip.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the length of the barrel and the rounded wooden grip.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and round with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_action_mechanism: none visible (revolver mechanism, not a shotgun action)
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized brass guard with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
